Name: Oddish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its scientific name is “Oddium Wanderus.” At night, it is said to walk nearly 1,000 feet on its two roots.

Name: Gloom
Flavor-Text: The honey it drools from its mouth smells so atrocious, it can curl noses more than a mile away.

Name: Vileplume
Ability-Effect: Each player can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: It has the world’s largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

Name: Bellossom
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Move as many [G] Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: When the heavy rainfall season ends, it is drawn out by warm sunlight to dance in the open.

Name: Spinarak
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

Name: Ariados
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this Ability. Both Active Pokémon (except for [G] Pokémon) are now Poisoned.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.

Name: Sceptile-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 70 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Sceptile-EX
Effects: You may attach up to 2 [G] Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal all damage from that Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.

Name: Combee
Flavor-Text: It collects and delivers honey to its colony. At night, they cluster to form a beehive and sleep.

Name: Vespiquen
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by Combee.

Name: Vespiquen
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.
Flavor-Text: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by Combee.

Name: Virizion
Effects: Put 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
If you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Legends say this Pokémon confounded opponents with its swift movements.

Name: Flareon
Ability-Effect: Each of your Stage 1 Pokémon in play is now a [R] Pokémon in addition to its existing types.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a flame bag inside its body. After inhaling deeply, it blows out flames of nearly 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Entei
Effects: Discard the top 4 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are [R] Energy cards, attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

Name: Entei
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona’s fire provided a replacement for the sun.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona’s fire provided a replacement for the sun.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on each of your Benched Magikarp.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.
Flavor-Text: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

Name: Vaporeon
Ability-Effect: Each of your Stage 1 Pokémon in play is now a [W] Pokémon in addition to its existing types.
Flavor-Text: It has evolved to be suitable for an aquatic life. It can invisibly melt away into water.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

Name: Regice
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of ice from the ice age. It controls frigid air of -328 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Kyurem-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jolteon
Ability-Effect: Each of your Stage 1 Pokémon in play is now a [L] Pokémon in addition to its existing types.
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It accumulates negative ions in the atmosphere to blast out 10,000-volt lightning bolts.

Name: Ampharos-EX
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and attach as many [L] Energy cards you find there as you like to this Pokémon. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Ampharos-EX
Effects: You may do 50 more damage and leave your opponent’s Active Pokémon Paralyzed. If you do, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Unown
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may discard this Pokémon and all cards attached to it (this does not count as a Knock Out). If you do, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Baltoy
Flavor-Text: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of either player’s deck and put them back on top of that player’s deck in any order.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

Name: Claydol
Effects: Devolve each of your opponent’s evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said that it originates from clay dolls made by an ancient civilization.

Name: Golett
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Ancient science fashioned this Pokémon from clay. It’s been active for thousands of years.

Name: Golurk
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [P] and [F] type.
Effects: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

Name: Hoopa-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon-EX (except for Hoopa-EX), reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Machamp-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Confused.
If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wooper
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the temperature cools in the evening, they emerge from water to seek food along the shore.

Name: Quagsire
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: This carefree Pokémon has an easy-going nature. While swimming, it always bumps into boat hulls.

Name: Regirock
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The same rocks that form its body have been found in ground layers around the world.

Name: Golurk
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a [F] Energy card, attach it to this Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

Name: Tyranitar-EX
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Tyranitar-EX
Effects: This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Choose a Supporter card from your opponent’s discard pile and use it as the effect of this attack.
Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

Name: Inkay
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent’s will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

Name: Malamar
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It lures prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids.

Name: Beldum
Flavor-Text: It converses with others by magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

Name: Metang
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its two brains are linked, amplifying its psychic power.

Name: Metagross
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, If you do, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard all [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

Name: Metagross
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each card in your hand.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.
Flavor-Text: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

Name: Registeel
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, discard an Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is said to be harder than any kind of metal. A study has revealed that its body is hollow.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Gardevoir
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to predict the future. Its power peaks when it is protecting its Trainer.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Perhaps because they feel more at ease in a group, they stick to others they find. They end up looking like a cloud.

Name: Whimsicott
Effects: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

Name: Giratina-EX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Mega Evolution Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Pokémon Tool, Special Energy, or Stadium cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Goomy
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, you may search your deck for Goomy and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It’s covered in a slimy membrane that makes any punches or kicks slide off it harmlessly.

Name: Sliggoo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.

Name: Goodra
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each [C] in its Retreat Cost. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Flavor-Text: It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that’s the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers.

Name: Meowth
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Name: Persian
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: Porygon
Effects: Look through your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that consists entirely of programming code. It is capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

Name: Porygon2
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: With planetary development software installed, it became capable of working in space.

Name: Porygon-Z
Effects: Discard all Special Energy attached to each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Its programming was modified to enable it to travel through alien dimensions. Seems there might have been an error…

Name: Porygon-Z
Effects: Devolve as many of your Benched Pokémon as many times as you like. Put each Evolution card removed this way into your hand.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its programming was modified to enable it to travel through alien dimensions. Seems there might have been an error…

Name: Lugia-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 70 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ace Trainer
Description: You can play this card only if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent.Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, draw 6 cards. Your opponent draws 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ampharos Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Ampharos-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Eco Arm
Description: Shuffle 3 Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Recycler
Description: Shuffle 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Faded Town
Description: At any time between turns, put 2 damage counters on each Mega Evolution Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Forest of Giant Plants
Description: Each player’s [G] Pokémon can evolve during his or her first turn or the turn he or she plays those Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Hex Maniac
Description: Until the end of your opponent’s next turn, each Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hex Maniac
Description: Until the end of your opponent’s next turn, each Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Level Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon with 90 HP or less, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lucky Helmet
Description: Whenever the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), draw 2 cards.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Lysandre
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Paint Roller
Description: Discard any Stadium card in play. Then, draw a card.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Sceptile Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Sceptile-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Tyranitar Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Tyranitar-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Dangerous Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [D] Pokémon. This card provides [D] Energy only while this card is attached to a [D] Pokémon.Whenever the [D] Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon-EX (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon-EX.(If this card is attached to anything other than a [D] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Flash Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [L] Pokémon. This card provides [L] Energy only while this card is attached to a [L] Pokémon.The [L] Pokémon this card is attached to has no Weakness.(If this card is attached to anything other than a [L] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Steven
Description: Search your deck for a Supporter card and a basic Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Primal Kyogre-EX
Effects: Move 2 Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon-EX. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Primal-Reversion-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Kyogre-EX, your turn ends.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes this Pokémon, heal all damage from it.

Name: Primal Groudon-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Primal-Reversion-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Groudon-EX, your turn ends.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes this Pokémon, heal all damage from it.

Name: M Rayquaza-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage times the number of your Benched Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes this Pokémon, heal all damage from it.

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Trainers’ Mail
Description: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Trainer card you find there (except for Trainers’ Mail) and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
